elements.sent-2411-001: The thermodynamic limits of the XYZ spin chain with periodic or twisted boundary conditions are studied.
elements.sent-2411-002: By using the technique of characterizing the eigenvalue of the transfer matrix by the $T-Q$ relation and by the zeros of the associated polynomial, we obtain the constraints of the Bethe roots and the zeros for the eigenvalues.
elements.sent-2411-003: With the help of structure of Bethe roots, we obtain the distribution patterns of zeros.
elements.sent-2411-004: Based on them, the physical quantities such as the surface energy and excitation energy are calculated.
elements.sent-2411-005: We find that both of them depend on the parity of sites number due to the topological long-range Neel order on the Mobius manifold in the spin space.
elements.sent-2411-006: We also check our results with those obtaining by the density matrix renormalization group.
elements.sent-2411-007: The method provided in this paper can be applied to study the thermodynamic properties at the thermal equilibrium state with finite temperature.
elements.sent-2411-008: The quantum spin chains have many applications in the many-body theory, magnetic ordered materials, statistical mechanics, AdS/CFT duality, and non-linear science \cite{baxter2016exactly,korepin1993quantum,vsamaj2013introduction .}
elements.sent-2411-009: Based on the exact solution of spin chains, many believable results about the fractional excitation, spinons, quantum phase transitions, helix states, and boundary states are obtained.
elements.sent-2411-010: Another interesting progress is that by using the separation of variables for the quantum integrable systems, the correlation functions, form factors and norm in the certain spin chains are also calculated.
elements.sent-2411-011: It has been shown that these many-body correlations have the wonderful determinant expressions.
elements.sent-2411-012: Recently, the quantum spin chains and their exact solutions are used to study the evolution of some entangled initial states, quench dynamics, Non-Hermitian physics, quantum information and quantum computation.
elements.sent-2411-013: We should note that during the study of exact solutions, many famous methods treating the many-body interactions such as Bethe ansatz, $T-Q$ relation \cite{baxter1971eight,baxter1971one,baxter1972partition,baxter1972one , and quantum inverse scattering method \cite{takhtadzhan1979quantum, korepin1993quantum, sklyanin1978anharmonic are proposed.}}
elements.sent-2411-014: The XYZ spin chain is the general model quantifying the anisotropic couplings, and has many important applications in the quantum magnetism.
elements.sent-2411-015: Due to the complicated spin exchanging and the competition, the quantum states and phase diagram of XYZ model are very rich.
elements.sent-2411-016: To calculate the exact solution of the XYZ spin chain, people usually adopts the periodic or open boundary conditions.
elements.sent-2411-017: In fact, the anti-periodic boundary condition is also an important quantization scheme.
elements.sent-2411-018: In this case, the boundary spin along one direction at one side of the chain remains unchanged while the rest components are flipped, then are connected to the corresponding boundary spin components at the other side.
elements.sent-2411-019: Although the real space of the system is one dimension, the spin space is three dimensions.
elements.sent-2411-020: Thus the system can be regarded as a Mobius strip in the spin space.
elements.sent-2411-021: The twisted bond can be smoothly moved and the system has the topological translational invariant.
elements.sent-2411-022: We should note that the $U(1)$ symmetry of the system is broken.
elements.sent-2411-023: The integrability of the XYZ spin chain with anti-periodic boundary condition has been proved for many decades.
elements.sent-2411-024: However, how to write out the exact solution of the XYZ spin chain is a longstanding problem due to the $U(1)$ symmetry and the traditional methods are invalid.
elements.sent-2411-025: Until recently, the exact solution is obtained by using the off-diagonal Bethe ansatz (ODBA) \cite{cao2013off .}
elements.sent-2411-026: The main idea is as follows.
elements.sent-2411-027: The eigenvalue of transfer matrix is an elliptic polynomial, which must satisfy some constraints.
elements.sent-2411-028: According to the algebraic analysis, if the number of constraints equals to the order of the polynomial, then the eigenvalue can be determined completely.
elements.sent-2411-029: By introducing some inhomogeneous parameters and using the technique of fusion, we indeed obtain these constraints.
elements.sent-2411-030: Then, by constructing the inhomogeneous $T-Q$ relation, the value of the transfer matrix and the energy spectrum of the anti-periodic XYZ spin chain are obtained \cite{cao2014spin,cao2013off-xyz,wang2015off .}
elements.sent-2411-031: The next problem is to study the thermodynamic limit, because the particle number in actual materials is proportional to the Avogadro constant.
elements.sent-2411-032: However, the Bethe ansatz equations in the anti-periodic XYZ spin chain are inhomogeneous, and we can not take the logarithm.
elements.sent-2411-033: The traditional thermodynamic Bethe ansatz (TBA) is invalid again.
elements.sent-2411-034: In order to overcome this difficulty, some methods are suggested.
elements.sent-2411-035: For example, the thermodynamic limit can be studied by using the degenerate points of model parameters where the Bethe ansatz equations are reduced to the homogeneous ones \cite{li2014thermodynamic,xin2020thermodynamic , the finite size scaling analysis of the inhomogeneous term in the $T-Q$ relation where the related contribution can be neglected if the number of sites tends to infinity \cite{qiao2018twisted,xin2018thermodynamic,sun2019surface , and the $t-W$ scheme where the eigenvalue of transfer matrix are parameterized by the zeros of the corresponding polynomial \cite{qiao2020exact,qiao2021exact,le2021root .}}}
elements.sent-2411-036: In this paper, we study the thermodynamic limit of the XYZ spin chain with twisted boundary condition.
elements.sent-2411-037: By comprehensive analyzing the structure of the Bethe roots and the zeros of the polynomial, we obtain the distribution patterns.
elements.sent-2411-038: Based on them, we obtain the density of zeros in the thermodynamic limit and study the physical quantities such as the twisted energy and elementary excitation.
elements.sent-2411-039: We find that the twisted and excitation energies depend on the parity of sites number due to the quasi-long-range Neel order in the system.
elements.sent-2411-040: These results are the foundations to study the thermodynamic properties at the thermal equilibrium state.
elements.sent-2411-041: Starting from these results, we can construct the quantum transfer matrix.
elements.sent-2411-042: By using the contour integral, we can calculate the thermodynamic quantities such as free energy, entropy, specific heat and magnetic susceptibility.
elements.sent-2411-043: The paper is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-044: In the next section, the model Hamiltonian and exact solution are introduced.
elements.sent-2411-045: In section 3, we calculate the thermodynamic limit of the system with real crossing parameter $\eta$.
elements.sent-2411-046: In section 4, we consider the pure imaginary $\eta$ case.
elements.sent-2411-047: The concluding remarks and discussions are provided in section 5.
elements.sent-2411-048: In the appendix, we list some useful identities of the elliptic functions.

elements.sent-2411-049: The boundary conditions are achieved by \begin{equation}\label{bc} \sigma^{x}_{N+1}= \sigma^{\beta}_{1} \sigma^{x}_{1} \sigma^{\beta}_{1},\quad \sigma^{y}_{N+1}= \sigma^{\beta}_{1} \sigma^{y}_{1} \sigma^{\beta}_{1},\quad \sigma^{z}_{N+1}= \sigma^{\beta}_{1} \sigma^{z}_{1} \sigma^{\beta}_{1}, \quad \beta=0,x,y,z,\end{equation} where $\beta=0$ represents the periodic boundary condition ($\sigma^{0}$ is the $2\times 2$ identity matrix), while $\beta=x$, $\beta=y$ and $\beta=z$ represent the twisted boundary condition obtained by twisting the $\pi$-angle around $x$-, $y$- and $z$-axis, respectively.

elements.sent-2411-322: In this paper, we investigated the thermodynamic limits of the XYZ spin chain with the periodic or the twisted boundary conditions.
elements.sent-2411-323: Based on the new scheme of parameterization of eigenvalue of the transfer matrix by the zeros instead of Bethe roots, we obtain analytically the ground state energy density, surface energy and the excitation energy.
elements.sent-2411-324: We find that these physical quantities depend on the parity of the system-size $N$ and the boundary conditions, due to the different easy-axis and the anisotropic couplings.
elements.sent-2411-325: For the real $\eta\in(0,1)$, the spins in the Neel state and in the low-lying states prefer to be arranged along the $x$-direction because it is the easy-axis.
elements.sent-2411-326: Thus the surface energy induced by the twisted boundary condition along the $x$-direction has the same parity dependence as that for the ground state energy of the periodic system.
elements.sent-2411-327: This dependence is broken by the twisted boundaries along the $y$- and $z$-directions.
elements.sent-2411-328: For the pure imaginary $\Im(\eta) \in (0,\frac{\tau {i )$, the easy-axis is the $z$-direction.}}
elements.sent-2411-329: The spins in the Neel state and in the low-lying states prefer to be arranged along the $z$-direction.
elements.sent-2411-330: Thus the energies in the thermodynamic limit induced by the twisted boundary condition along the $z$-direction have the same parity dependence as that for the periodic case.
elements.sent-2411-331: This dependence is broken by the twisted boundaries along the $x$- and $y$-directions.
